title: galium commune var. fleurotii
intro: Galium commune var. fleurotii, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial herb belonging to the bedstraw family, native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial agent, due to the presence of essential oils like borneol and linalool. The health benefits of Woodruff include its potential to improve digestion, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Woodruff can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb for use in herbal remedies.
